design_philosophy.core_loop: Score more points than opponent within time limit; offensive execution (dribble, pass, shoot with timing) vs defensive reaction (steal, block, rebound); character-specific movement properties affect all actions
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure combined with opponent scaling difficulty; resource management of player stamina/positioning; execution-heavy shooting and dunking mechanics requiring precise button timing
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control a five-man basketball team in fast-paced arcade basketball matches; perform cinematic slam dunks with timing-based execution; learn and master character movement differences
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavy emphasis on presentation (cinematic dunk animations uncommon for 1987) and character animation over statistical simulation; arcade-oriented design with continuous difficulty scaling rather than discrete levels; movement mechanics deeply tied to individual character properties requiring learned mastery

difficulty.continues_system: Coin insertion system with one-minute credit timer; earning points extends playtime, establishing soft-continue mechanic
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty scales continuously with game duration (longer play = harder opponent); player choice of character affects execution difficulty (different jump heights, speeds, movement timing requirements); no explicit difficulty settings documented

progression.unlock_mechanism: Game progression tied to score threshold; earning more points than opponent extends playtime by one minute, creating natural progression gates
replayability_hooks: Character mastery (learning individual movement properties and jump timings)
Score attacks (continuous difficulty scaling rewards high performance)
Two-player competitive mode (symmetric ruleset for head-to-head play)
Skill-based execution (timing windows for shots and dunks create consistent replay value)

world_building_techniques: Visual presentation of basketball arena and players
Cinematic dunk animations establishing era-defining presentation

environmental_storytelling: Court layout and player positioning communicate team roles
Character animation qualities (jump height, speed) establish player archetypes

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Double Dribble (NES, Famicom Disk System) article
